# Jamie Simon

> Personal site of Jamie Simon — ML researcher and writer. Posts span machine learning theory, physics, math, music, and general curiosity. Based in Berkeley.

## Research
- [Research overview](https://jamiesimon.io/research/)

## Interactive demos
- [Shallow MLP Gym](https://jamiesimon.io/shallow-mlps/) — train two-layer MLPs interactively in the browser
- [t-SNE explorer](https://jamiesimon.io/t-sne/)
- [Expander nets](https://jamiesimon.io/expander-nets/)
- [Low-dim flows](https://jamiesimon.io/low-dim-flows/)
- [Puzzles](https://jamiesimon.io/puzzles/)

## ML theory posts
- [The polynomial features of random neural networks](https://jamiesimon.io/blog/polynomial-features-random-networks/)
- [Predicting kernel spectra powerlaw exponents from raw data statistics](https://jamiesimon.io/blog/kernel-spectral-decay-powerlaw/)
- [A complete characterization of the expressivity of shallow, bias-free ReLU networks](https://jamiesimon.io/blog/the-expressivity-of-shallow-relu-nets/)
- [Backsolving classical generalization bounds from the modern kernel regression eigenframework](https://jamiesimon.io/blog/backsolving-classical-bounds/)
- [One kernel, many eigensystems](https://jamiesimon.io/blog/one-kernel-many-eigensystems/)
- [The eigensystem of the Gaussian kernel w.r.t. a Gaussian measure](https://jamiesimon.io/blog/gaussian-kernel-1d-eigendecomp/)
- [A theory of generalization for wide neural nets (eigenlearning)](https://jamiesimon.io/blog/eigenlearning/)
- [An eigenframework for the generalization of 1NN on the torus](https://jamiesimon.io/blog/1nn-eigenframework/)
- [Infinite-width autoencoders are cursed](https://jamiesimon.io/blog/autoencoders-are-cursed/)
- [The optimal low-rank solution for multivariate linear regression](https://jamiesimon.io/blog/low-rank-linear-regression-sol/)
- [It's hard to turn a low-rank matrix into a high-rank matrix](https://jamiesimon.io/blog/transforming-low-rank-matrices/)
- [Let's solve more learning rules](https://jamiesimon.io/blog/lets-solve-learning-rules/)
- [Reverse engineering the NTK](https://jamiesimon.io/blog/reverse-engineering/)
- [Multiplicative neural networks](https://jamiesimon.io/blog/multiplicative-neural-nets/)
- [Insights into GPT-2's positional encodings](https://jamiesimon.io/blog/gpt2-positional-encs/)
- [Creating and erasing AI watermarks](https://jamiesimon.io/blog/llm-watermarking/)

## Math and physics posts
- [Using the Laplacian to take a local average of a function](https://jamiesimon.io/blog/the-laplacian-and-diffusion/)
- [Modeling time-reversed random walks](https://jamiesimon.io/blog/time-reversed-random-walks/)
- [Number theory, fractals, and crystallography in rows of crops](https://jamiesimon.io/blog/cropland-crystallography/)
- [Understanding fractals from iterated maps](https://jamiesimon.io/blog/fractals-in-iterated-maps/)
- [The principle of least power dissipation](https://jamiesimon.io/blog/least_power_dissipation/)
- [On the scientific method and its application to the science of deep learning](https://jamiesimon.io/blog/on-the-scientific-method/)

## Science and curiosity posts
- [What would happen if you made a planet out of fish?](https://jamiesimon.io/blog/fish-planet/)
- [How hard do you have to hit a chicken to cook it?](https://jamiesimon.io/blog/chicken-cooking/)
- [Could you propel a spacecraft using sports projectiles?](https://jamiesimon.io/blog/rocketball/)
- [How would an upside-down candle burn?](https://jamiesimon.io/blog/upside-down-candle/)
- [The expected cost of breaking quarantine](https://jamiesimon.io/blog/covid-calculation/)
- [Can a chemical reaction measure the size of its container?](https://jamiesimon.io/blog/volumetric-reaction/)
- [Ibuprofen vs. aspirin vs naproxen vs acetaminophen](https://jamiesimon.io/blog/otc-pain-relievers/)
- [How many babies were born during the 2024 eclipse?](https://jamiesimon.io/blog/eclipse-baby/)
- [Simulating cells fighting to the death](https://jamiesimon.io/blog/cell-fight/)
- [Experiments in self-assembly](https://jamiesimon.io/blog/self-assembly/)
- [Reflections on introductory neuroscience reading](https://jamiesimon.io/blog/neuro-learnings/)
